meta.schema: clubs.{club}.{meta,stats,inverseMap}; cluster adds speedHist, carryM{p10,p50,p90}, alsoProduces(9-flight), startLineMix, representative.spinLoft
meta.driverEngineNote: impact-flight.js has no driver preset; club:'driver' falls back to the 7-iron preset (VERIFIED identical). Driver grid = shared physics through driver-realistic input ranges; bands are percentile-anchored to the driver grid's OWN distribution; absolute driver carry compresses at high speed and is never treated as truth.
meta.conditioningModel: posterior(cluster) ∝ prior(cluster) × P(answer|cluster); P(answer|cluster) = cluster member fraction in the answered band (speedHist / carry-CDF / startLineMix) or a hard attack-band filter (divot). Renormalize to 100 after each answer.

baseline.note: well-struck 7-iron: square, ~-3° descending, ~28° delivered loft — fixed reference per clubSpeed, not re-optimized

distance.lossNote: Geometry alone moves this club's carry only -8.8%..4.9% (finding #1). The worst "Noticeable loss" band is just 2.3%..4.9% of carry — the S3 UI word "Way short" names the PERCEIVED miss, but a loss that large is strike or club speed, which is exactly why "Way short" promotes the contact card (spec §3.4). The data never overstates the geometry loss.

anchorCopy.slow: Gentle 7-iron speed — most of these carry around 106 m. Common for juniors, seniors and newer players (under 75 mph).
anchorCopy.mid: The middle of the club-golfer range — around 145 m carry. Where most amateurs sit (75–95 mph).
anchorCopy.fast: Strong / low-handicap speed — around 185 m carry or more (95 mph and up).

baseline.note: neutral driver strike: square, ~+2° up, ~13° delivered loft (near the low-spin-loft optimum) — fixed reference per clubSpeed, not re-optimized

distance.lossNote: Geometry alone moves this club's carry only -0.4%..4.9% (finding #1). The worst "Noticeable loss" band is just 2.5%..4.9% of carry — the S3 UI word "Way short" names the PERCEIVED miss, but a loss that large is strike or club speed, which is exactly why "Way short" promotes the contact card (spec §3.4). The data never overstates the geometry loss.

anchorCopy.slow: Gentle Driver speed — a senior, junior, lady or newer player's swing (under 90 mph).
anchorCopy.mid: The middle of the amateur driver range (90–105 mph) — where most club golfers sit.
anchorCopy.fast: Strong / low-handicap driver speed (105 mph and up).

speed.note: StrikeArc models the driver on the engine's shared 7-iron physics (no dedicated driver preset yet), so it reads the shape and cause of your miss — not an exact driver carry. The model's driver carry runs short of real-world yardage at these speeds, so no absolute carry is quoted here; the diagnosis and every band are relative to the driver grid's own distribution and stay correct.
